---
description: "Generate a comprehensive meeting prep package for an upcoming meeting. Use when: preparing for a call or in-person meeting with a target account. Pulls meeting brief, persona intel, and account context to create a one-page battle card."
---

# Meeting Prep — {{account}}

## Instructions

1. **Pull all context** for this account:
   - Meeting brief: why this account, why now, likely pain, primo relevance, best outcome, open questions
   - Persona details: name, title, priorities, role in deal
   - Prior activities and outreach history
   - Any email engagement data (what did they open/click?)

2. **Generate a battle card**:

```
## Meeting Battle Card: [Account]
**Date:** [date] | **Persona:** [name, title] | **Format:** [call/in-person/MODEX]

### 30-Second Opener
[Personalized opening that references their world — new role, company initiative, MODEX context]

### Their Likely Pain (say this back to them)
- [Pain point 1 — specific to their vertical/role]
- [Pain point 2]

### Our Proof Points
- $1M+ profit lift per facility (average across pilots)
- Drop-and-hook time: 48 min → 24 min
- Zero headcount additions required
- [Account-specific proof if available]

### Questions to Ask (listen more than talk)
1. [Open question from meeting brief]
2. "What does your yard process look like today?"
3. "If you could fix one thing about dock scheduling, what would it be?"

### Best Possible Outcome
[From meeting brief — what does success look like from this meeting?]

### Next Step to Propose
"Can we show you a 15-minute demo of how [similar company] solved this?"
or
"Can I send you our audit framework? Takes 10 minutes, shows exactly where time leaks."

### Red Lines / Landmines
- [Any sensitivities — competitor relationships, bad timing, political dynamics]
- Dannon: If this is Dannon, this meeting MUST have come through Mark Shaughnessy
```
